title: Added format parameter to sync command

comments.body:
> When copying image1 to `repo:tag` and then copying image2 to `repo:tag`, the `tag` is removed from image1

Thanks. That looks entirely expected in the container registry model: If a client later requests to download `repo:tag`, it will expect to get image2 and never image1; so the `repo:tag` is no longer pointing to image1, i.e. that it was “removed” from image1 in a sense.

So that is unrelated to the need for this option?

---

> When I attempt to push a multi-arch image using `-all` parameter in `skopeo sync` it fails on Harbor < 2.x.

A schema2 image fails by default but it does work with a manual `--format` ? How does that fail? The code is supposed to _automatically_ try various formats. If there is a bug in that code, we should fix it if possible, rather than force users to manually use an option.

> With Harbor 2.x and their support for OCI manifests, it appears to correctly infer the MIME type and uses an OCI manifest by default to support multi-arch.

Or are you saying that all multi-arch pushes fail on Harbor < 2.x, and they automatically work without the `--format` option on ≥ 2.x? So the option is not _necessary_ to make sync work?

If the multi-arch code should work by default but doesn’t fall back correctly, please share a `skopeo --debug sync` output (maybe in the issue, not in this PR).

If this option is not required for the sync to work, it might still make sense to provide it, I just want to make sure we are not ignoring a bug that should be fixed.

> > When copying image1 to `repo:tag` and then copying image2 to `repo:tag`, the `tag` is removed from image1
> 
> Thanks. That looks entirely expected in the container registry model: If a client later requests to download `repo:tag`, it will expect to get image2 and never image1; so the `repo:tag` is no longer pointing to image1, i.e. that it was “removed” from image1 in a sense.
> 
> So that is unrelated to the need for this option?
> 
> > When I attempt to push a multi-arch image using `-all` parameter in `skopeo sync` it fails on Harbor < 2.x.
> 
> A schema2 image fails by default but it does work with a manual `--format` ? How does that fail? The code is supposed to _automatically_ try various formats. If there is a bug in that code, we should fix it if possible, rather than force users to manually use an option.
> 
> > With Harbor 2.x and their support for OCI manifests, it appears to correctly infer the MIME type and uses an OCI manifest by default to support multi-arch.
> 
> Or are you saying that all multi-arch pushes fail on Harbor < 2.x, and they automatically work without the `--format` option on ≥ 2.x? So the option is not _necessary_ to make sync work?
> 
> If the multi-arch code should work by default but doesn’t fall back correctly, please share a `skopeo --debug sync` output (maybe in the issue, not in this PR).
> 
> If this option is not required for the sync to work, it might still make sense to provide it, I just want to make sure we are not ignoring a bug that should be fixed.

Yeah looking like I'm just an idiot.  Not sure what problem I was having initially with Harbor 2.x, but doing a normal `sync --all` from docker hub to Harbor 2.x works fine, automatically determines OCI manifest format, and uploads the properly tagged multi-arch image without any issues.

Harbor 1.x does not support multi-arch image manifests and fails as expected with `Manifest.list is not supported` response

I'm happy to finish up this PR or it can be closed if you don't see a reason to add the param to `sync`.  Doesn't look like I need it for my purposes as originally thought.

Thanks very much for the update.

> I'm happy to finish up this PR or it can be closed if you don't see a reason to add the param to `sync`.

Up to you, I don’t feel strongly about this at all. As the above shows, sometimes the discussion about the option can be more helpful than the existence of the option :)

OTOH it’s certainly attractive for `copy` and `sync` to behave very similarly and to provide substantially the same tunables.
I like the idea of the option.

Added basic integration test for `sync --format`

Let me know if anything else is needed for this PR

> Let me know if anything else is needed for this PR

Completions (discussed elsewhere) and an update to `docs/skopeo-sync.1.md`, I think. Code looks good.

> Completions (discussed elsewhere) and an update to `docs/skopeo-sync.1.md`, I think. Code looks good.

Added all completions for sync.  

What update is needed for `docs/skopeo-sync.1.md`?  I added the format parameter previously and corrected the language regarding the `dir:` restrictions based on previous feedback.

Looks like `validate` is failing on `gofmt` due to wanting to sort imports.  Sorted imports don't look like are being used in the current HEAD of master.

Should I add a commit with sorted imports?

> What update is needed for `docs/skopeo-sync.1.md`? I added the format parameter previously and corrected the language regarding the `dir:` restrictions based on previous feedback.

I’m sorry, I must have mistakenly viewed only one of the commits or something.

> Looks like `validate` is failing on `gofmt` due to wanting to sort imports. Sorted imports don't look like are being used in the current HEAD of master.

The failure says
> `Please reformat the above files using "gofmt -s -w" and commit the result.`

and that should be all that is required. (The imports are sorted by package name, not purely line-by-line, and only within the empty-line-delineated sections. So removing an empty line can change the order.)

> Should I add a commit with sorted imports?

It might be easier to squash all the commits into one, including the changes made by the `gofmt` step.

> > Looks like `validate` is failing on `gofmt` due to wanting to sort imports. Sorted imports don't look like are being used in the current HEAD of master.
> 
> The failure says
> 
> > `Please reformat the above files using "gofmt -s -w" and commit the result.`
> 
> and that should be all that is required. (The imports are sorted by package name, not purely line-by-line, and only within the empty-line-delineated sections. So removing an empty line can change the order.)
> 
> > Should I add a commit with sorted imports?
> 
> It might be easier to squash all the commits into one, including the changes made by the `gofmt` step.

Fixed docs and help language and squashed commits
